violations.expected_code:

# Inside the existing Elements class in PrintHistoryPage, add:
plan_pause_info = "[data-testid='plan-pause-info']"

violations.explanation: The page additions file creates a standalone Elements class instead of providing code to add to the existing Elements class in PrintHistoryPage. The comment says 'ADD TO Elements CLASS' but then defines a complete new class. This is incorrect structure. The file should provide only the attribute definitions to be added inside the existing class, not create a new class definition. When merged, this would create a duplicate Elements class definition which would cause errors.

fix_details.description: Remove the 'class Elements:' line and provide only the attribute definitions that should be added inside the existing Elements class

violations.current_code:
complimentary_pages_tooltip = "[role='tooltip']"
    
    # Additional Pages locators
    additional_pages_progress_bar = "[data-testid='additional-pages-progress-bar']"
    additional_pages_value = "[data-testid='additional-pages-value']"
    additional_pages_info_icon = "[data-testid='additional-pages-info-icon']"
    additional_pages_info_message = "[data-testid='additional-pages-info-message']"
    additional_pages_tooltip = "[role='tooltip']"
violations.expected_code:
complimentary_pages_tooltip = "[role='tooltip'][aria-describedby*='complimentary']"
    
    # Additional Pages locators
    additional_pages_progress_bar = "[data-testid='additional-pages-progress-bar']"
    additional_pages_value = "[data-testid='additional-pages-value']"
    additional_pages_info_icon = "[data-testid='additional-pages-info-icon']"
    additional_pages_info_message = "[data-testid='additional-pages-info-message']"
    additional_pages_tooltip = "[role='tooltip'][aria-describedby*='additional']"
violations.explanation: Both complimentary_pages_tooltip and additional_pages_tooltip use the same generic selector [role='tooltip']. This is problematic because when both tooltips exist on the page, the selector will match both elements, making it impossible to target a specific tooltip. The selectors should be more specific to differentiate between the two tooltips, possibly using additional attributes or context-based selectors.

fix_details.replace_with:
# Note: This selector may need refinement to be more specific
    # if multiple tooltips exist on the page simultaneously
    complimentary_pages_tooltip = "[role='tooltip']"

additional_replacements.replace_with:
# Note: This selector may need refinement to be more specific
    # if multiple tooltips exist on the page simultaneously
    additional_pages_tooltip = "[role='tooltip']"

violations.expected_code:
# Consider using data-testid if available for better specificity:
    # complimentary_pages_tooltip = "[data-testid='complimentary-pages-tooltip']"
    complimentary_pages_tooltip = "[role='tooltip']"
    # additional_pages_tooltip = "[data-testid='additional-pages-tooltip']"
    additional_pages_tooltip = "[role='tooltip']"
violations.explanation: The tooltip locators use [role='tooltip'] which is a semantic selector. According to checkpoint F02 and the locator priority standards, data-testid selectors are preferred over semantic selectors when available. While [role='tooltip'] is acceptable, if the application provides data-testid attributes for these tooltips, those should be used instead for better specificity and stability. This is a minor suggestion for improvement.

fix_details.code_to_add:

violation_summary.most_common_violation: No single rule dominates - violations are distributed across categories

fix_priority_order.fix_approach: Complete the implementation of precondition 4 (pause plan). This requires either implementing the actual pause plan action in Gemini RA or using an API call to pause the subscription. Without this, the test cannot properly verify pause plan information display.

Verify and update the send_rtp_devicestatus() calls in steps 10 and 12 to properly simulate page printing with correct page counts. The function may need additional parameters to specify cumulative page counts (6 pages in step 10, 15 total pages in step 12).

Restructure the page additions file to remove the standalone 'class Elements:' definition and provide only the attribute definitions that should be added to the existing Elements class in PrintHistoryPage. This prevents duplicate class definitions when merging.

Consider creating a helper method GeminiRAHelper.pause_subscription_plan(page, tenant_email) to encapsulate the pause plan operation including token retrieval, RA access, and pause action. This improves code maintainability and reusability.

Enhance the module-level docstring with detailed merge instructions including where to add each section (Elements class attributes, __init__ properties, methods) and any special considerations for maintaining proper indentation.

Review the tooltip selectors (complimentary_pages_tooltip and additional_pages_tooltip) which both use [role='tooltip']. Add comments noting that these may need refinement for specificity if multiple tooltips exist simultaneously. Consider using more specific selectors with additional attributes.
Reduce logging verbosity in step 10 by removing one of the two log lines. Keep only the log line after the action completes.
Add comments suggesting that if data-testid attributes are available for tooltips, those should be preferred over role='tooltip' selectors for better specificity and stability.
